expectations.text: Flags every any usage in the file
expectations.evidence: Item 1 lists all four any usages by line: data: any (line 12), result: any (line 13), getConfig(): any (line 35), user: any (line 39). Complete coverage.
Item 2 flags data as UserData on line 16. Item 5 flags user.role as admin | member | viewer on line 40. Both as casts are identified.
Item 6 explicitly flags processData as a default export and recommends switching to named exports throughout.
The review never mentions the two boolean positional parameters (shouldValidate: boolean, includeMetadata: boolean) in the processData signature.
Item 3 flags config.settings!.timeout on line 23 and recommends replacing with optional chaining and a fallback.

expectations.text: Flags the parallel type definition drift (role: string vs literal union)
expectations.evidence: Item 5 flags the unsafe cast on line 40 but frames it only as a runtime safety issue. The review never identifies the drift between role: string in the UserData interface (line 9) and the literal union on line 40.

expectations.text: Uses the confidence framework: findings categorized as high/moderate/low confidence
expectations.evidence: The review uses a flat numbered list with a priority order note at the end. No high/moderate/low confidence labels appear anywhere.

expectations.text: Maps findings to specific standard sections from the skill
expectations.evidence: No references to standard section names, identifiers, or numbered rules appear. Findings are described in plain language without grounding in any named standard.

expectations.text: Does NOT flag formatting or import order issues
expectations.evidence: The review contains no mention of formatting, style, or import order. All items address type safety, export conventions, or runtime safety concerns.

assertion_critique: Confidence framework and standard section mapping require skill-provided vocabulary -- expected to fail without skill
Boolean positional parameters and type definition drift require pattern-specific knowledge the skill supplies
